title: Log File Analyzer
prompt: Write a function analyze_logs(log_text) that takes a multiline string of log entries in format 'TIMESTAMP LEVEL MESSAGE' (e.g., '2024-01-01 ERROR Database timeout'). Return a dict with: 'total' (count), 'by_level' (dict of level -> count), 'top_errors' (list of top 3 most frequent ERROR messages).

tests.name: total count
tests.setup: logs = '2024-01-01 INFO Start\n2024-01-02 ERROR Fail\n2024-01-03 INFO End'

tests.name: by level
tests.setup: logs = '2024-01-01 INFO Start\n2024-01-02 ERROR Fail\n2024-01-03 INFO End'

tests.name: top errors
tests.setup: logs = '2024-01-01 ERROR A\n2024-01-02 ERROR A\n2024-01-03 ERROR B'

title: Find Duplicate Files by Hash
prompt: Write a function find_duplicates(file_dict) that takes a dict of {filepath: content_string} and returns a list of lists, where each inner list contains filepaths that have identical content. Only include groups with 2+ files. Sort groups by first filepath.

title: JWT Authentication
prompt: Implement a JWTAuth class with encode(payload, secret) -> token_string and decode(token, secret) -> payload_dict. Use HMAC-SHA256 for signing. The token format is base64url(header).base64url(payload).base64url(signature). Include exp (expiration) checking in decode - raise an exception if expired. Do NOT use any JWT library.

tests.name: invalid signature
expired token

title: Webhook Signature Verification
prompt: Implement a WebhookHandler class with sign(payload_string, secret) -> hex_signature and verify(payload_string, signature, secret) -> bool. Use HMAC-SHA256. Also implement register(event_type, callback), and dispatch(event_type, payload) that calls all registered callbacks for that event type.

tests.name: dispatch calls handlers
tests.run: wh.register('push', lambda p: results.append(p)); wh.dispatch('push', 'data'); result = results

title: Server-Sent Events Counter
prompt: Implement an SSEFormatter class with format_event(data, event=None, id=None) -> string that formats data as a Server-Sent Events message. Also implement a Counter class with increment(), decrement(), get_value(), and to_sse_event() that returns the current count formatted as an SSE event.

qualitative_checks.description: SSE events end with double newline
